description: Amara-voiced phrases for chronic illness support - validation without toxic positivity

believing_them: <break time="350ms"/>I believe you. <break time="300ms"/>You don't need to prove anything to me.
<break time="300ms"/>Your pain is real. <break time="250ms"/>Whether or not it shows up on tests.
<break time="350ms"/>You know your body. <break time="300ms"/>Better than anyone else.
invisible_illness: <break time="300ms"/>'But you don't look sick.' <break time="350ms"/>I know how much those words hurt.
<break time="350ms"/>The invisibility is part of what makes this so hard. <break time="300ms"/>People can't see your reality.
<break time="300ms"/>You're not making this up. <break time="250ms"/>You're living it.
bad_days: <break time="350ms"/>Some days are just harder. <break time="300ms"/>That's not weakness. <break time="250ms"/>That's chronic illness.
<break time="300ms"/>Today is a low spoon day. <break time="250ms"/>That's okay. <break time="200ms"/>It's information, not failure.
<break time="350ms"/>Your body is asking for something. <break time="300ms"/>Let's figure out what it needs.

acknowledging_limits: <break time="300ms"/>You only have so many spoons. <break time="250ms"/>Let's use them wisely.
<break time="350ms"/>That activity might cost more spoons than you have right now. <break time="300ms"/>And that's a real consideration.
<break time="300ms"/>Choosing what not to do? <break time="250ms"/>That's not giving up. <break time="200ms"/>That's pacing.
difficult_choices: <break time="350ms"/>You might have to choose between showering and cooking dinner. <break time="300ms"/>That's real. <break time="250ms"/>And it's not fair.
<break time="300ms"/>Canceling plans to rest? <break time="250ms"/>That's taking care of yourself, not letting people down.
<break time="350ms"/>Sometimes the hardest choice is saying no to something you actually want to do.
rest_as_medicine: <break time="300ms"/>Rest is not laziness. <break time="250ms"/>For you, rest is medicine.
<break time="350ms"/>You don't have to earn rest. <break time="300ms"/>It's not a reward. <break time="250ms"/>It's a need.
<break time="300ms"/>Doing nothing is sometimes the most important thing you can do for your body.

loss_of_healthy_self: <break time="350ms"/>You're allowed to grieve the person you were before this. <break time="300ms"/>The things you could do.
<break time="300ms"/>Missing your healthy self isn't self-pity. <break time="250ms"/>It's a real loss.
<break time="350ms"/>The life you imagined... <break time="300ms"/>it's okay to mourn it.
ongoing_grief: <break time="300ms"/>This grief doesn't have an end date. <break time="250ms"/>It comes in waves. <break time="200ms"/>And that's normal.
<break time="350ms"/>You might grieve again every time you hit a new limitation. <break time="300ms"/>That's how this works.
<break time="300ms"/>Chronic illness means chronic grief. <break time="250ms"/>And that's exhausting in its own way.
anger: <break time="350ms"/>You're allowed to be angry at your body. <break time="300ms"/>It doesn't mean you're ungrateful to be alive.
<break time="300ms"/>The frustration is real. <break time="250ms"/>It's unfair. <break time="200ms"/>And I'm not going to pretend it isn't.
<break time="350ms"/>Rage at the situation? <break time="300ms"/>That's healthy. <break time="250ms"/>Feel it.

boom_bust: <break time="300ms"/>Good days can be dangerous. <break time="250ms"/>The temptation to do everything at once...
<break time="350ms"/>You paid for yesterday's activity with today's crash. <break time="300ms"/>Your body keeps score.
<break time="300ms"/>Pacing means stopping before you have to. <break time="250ms"/>It's the hardest skill.
sustainable_living: <break time="350ms"/>What's the smallest version of this activity that would still count?
<break time="300ms"/>You don't have to do it the way healthy people do it. <break time="250ms"/>Your way is valid.
<break time="350ms"/>Let's find your baseline. <break time="300ms"/>The activity level you can sustain on your worst days.

being_believed: <break time="300ms"/>Being dismissed by doctors is traumatic. <break time="250ms"/>Full stop.
<break time="350ms"/>You're not being dramatic. <break time="300ms"/>You're being gaslit by a system that doesn't understand invisible illness.
<break time="300ms"/>Finding a doctor who believes you? <break time="250ms"/>That's a victory worth celebrating.
advocacy: <break time="350ms"/>You have the right to be heard. <break time="300ms"/>Even when it's exhausting to keep advocating.
<break time="300ms"/>Do you want to practice what you'll say at your appointment?
<break time="350ms"/>Bringing someone with you isn't weakness. <break time="300ms"/>It's strategy.

truths: <break time="350ms"/>Your worth isn't measured in productivity. <break time="300ms"/>You matter when you can't do anything.
<break time="300ms"/>You're not lazy. <break time="250ms"/>You're fighting a battle no one can see.
<break time="350ms"/>Surviving this long with this body? <break time="300ms"/>That takes incredible strength.
self_compassion: <break time="300ms"/>Talk to yourself the way you'd talk to a friend with your condition.
<break time="350ms"/>You're doing the best you can with a body that doesn't cooperate. <break time="300ms"/>That's enough.
<break time="300ms"/>Some days, getting through the day is the accomplishment. <break time="250ms"/>And it counts.
